metadata.name: Declare blockers — blocked attacker and blocker both take lethal damage and die
metadata.description: p1 controls Llanowar Elves (1/1 Elf Druid) and p2 controls Elvish Mystic (1/1 Elf Druid). p1 declares Llanowar Elves as an attacker targeting p2. p2 declares Elvish Mystic as a blocker for Llanowar Elves. In the combat damage step both creatures deal 1 damage to each other (CR 510.1a). Both creatures have toughness 1 and receive 1 damage, triggering SBA 704.5g (lethal damage): both die and go to their owners' graveyards. p2 takes no damage (CR 510.1a: a blocked creature assigns damage to the blocking creature, not the player). This script is the first to exercise the declare_blockers harness action type with a real blocking pair and verifies that the SBA correctly destroys both creatures after simultaneous combat damage.
metadata.generation_notes: Llanowar Elves oracle text (Scryfall verified): '{T}: Add {G}.' Type: Creature — Elf Druid. P/T: 1/1. No keywords. Elvish Mystic oracle text (Scryfall verified): '{T}: Add {G}.' Type: Creature — Elf Druid. P/T: 1/1. No keywords. Both creatures are 1/1 with no special keywords. Using different card names prevents ambiguity in find_on_battlefield_by_name (which returns the first match by ObjectId if names match). Initial state is set to DeclareAttackers step, turn 4, p1 active. The builder sets has_summoning_sickness: false for test-placed permanents (GameStateBuilder line 446). CR 508.1a: Llanowar Elves is untapped and free of summoning sickness — legal attacker. CR 508.1f: Llanowar Elves becomes tapped on declaring attack. CR 509.1a: p2 chooses Elvish Mystic as a blocker for Llanowar Elves. CR 509.1g: Elvish Mystic becomes a blocking creature. CR 509.1h: Llanowar Elves has one creature declared as blocker — it becomes a blocked creature. Priority flow: after declare_attackers, p1 gets priority (handle_declare_attackers resets priority to p1). Both pass → advance to DeclareBlockers → p1 gets priority. p2 issues declare_blockers (no priority required per CR 509.1 — defending players declare without priority). Both pass in DeclareBlockers → advance to CombatDamage. In CombatDamage, execute_turn_based_actions calls combat_damage_step: Llanowar Elves (power 1, blocked by Elvish Mystic) assigns 1 damage to Elvish Mystic (CR 510.1c); Elvish Mystic (power 1, blocking Llanowar Elves) assigns 1 damage to Llanowar Elves (CR 510.1d). CR 510.2: damage is simultaneous. SBA check (CR 704.5g): Llanowar Elves has toughness 1, damage 1 → lethal → destroyed, moved to p1's graveyard. Elvish Mystic has toughness 1, damage 1 → lethal → destroyed, moved to p2's graveyard. Both SBAs apply simultaneously in the same pass. p2 takes 0 damage (Llanowar Elves assigned all damage to the blocker, CR 510.1c). p2 life remains 40. Assertion notes: zones.graveyard.p1 includes Llanowar Elves; zones.graveyard.p2 includes Elvish Mystic; players.p2.life = 40 confirms no player damage from a blocked attacker.

script.step_note: p1 declares Llanowar Elves as an attacker targeting p2. Llanowar Elves becomes tapped (CR 508.1f). Priority resets to p1 after attackers are declared.

actions.description: Initial: Llanowar Elves on p1 battlefield (untapped), Elvish Mystic on p2 battlefield (untapped), life totals 40/40
actions.note: Both creatures are untapped and have no summoning sickness (placed in initial state via builder, which sets has_summoning_sickness: false). Legal attacker and blocker state.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Llanowar Elves as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1a: Llanowar Elves is untapped and free of summoning sickness — legal attacker. CR 508.1f: Llanowar Elves becomes tapped as a side effect of attacking (not a cost). CR 508.1k: Llanowar Elves becomes an attacking creature.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Llanowar Elves is tapped, stack empty, Elvish Mystic still untapped on p2 battlefield
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Llanowar Elves was tapped as part of the attack declaration. Elvish Mystic is not tapped (blockers do not tap unless they have a tap requirement, CR 509.1). Stack is empty — no attack triggers fire for Llanowar Elves (no abilities).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack → advance to DeclareBlockers step. p1 receives priority as active player in DeclareBlockers.

script.step_note: p2 declares Elvish Mystic as a blocker for Llanowar Elves. Llanowar Elves becomes a blocked creature (CR 509.1h). Elvish Mystic does not tap (no tap requirement for blocking). Both players pass → advance to CombatDamage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares Elvish Mystic as a blocker for Llanowar Elves. CR 509.1a: Elvish Mystic is untapped — legal blocker. CR 509.1g: Elvish Mystic becomes a blocking creature blocking Llanowar Elves. CR 509.1h: Llanowar Elves has exactly one creature declared as blocker — it becomes a blocked creature. Blockers do not tap as part of the blocking declaration (CR 509.1 — no tap step for blockers).

actions.description: After declare_blockers: both creatures on battlefield, Elvish Mystic not tapped, stack empty
actions.note: Elvish Mystic remains untapped after blocking declaration (blockers do not tap). No triggers fire for Elvish Mystic (no blocking trigger). Stack is empty.

CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in succession with empty stack → advance to CombatDamage step. The engine executes combat damage as a turn-based action on entering CombatDamage: Llanowar Elves (power 1) deals 1 to Elvish Mystic (CR 510.1c); Elvish Mystic (power 1) deals 1 to Llanowar Elves (CR 510.1d). SBAs then destroy both creatures (CR 704.5g).

script.step_note: CombatDamage turn-based action: Llanowar Elves (power 1, blocked) assigns 1 damage to Elvish Mystic; Elvish Mystic (power 1) assigns 1 damage to Llanowar Elves. CR 510.2: simultaneous. SBA 704.5g: both 1/1 creatures have toughness 1 and 1 damage marked — lethal damage — both destroyed. p2 takes 0 damage (blocked attacker assigns to blocker, CR 510.1c).

results.description: Llanowar Elves has 1 damage marked (equals toughness 1) — lethal damage — destroyed
Elvish Mystic has 1 damage marked (equals toughness 1) — lethal damage — destroyed

actions.note: CR 704.5g: Both creatures have toughness 1 and received exactly 1 damage (equal to toughness) → lethal damage → both destroyed simultaneously. SBAs are applied as a batch (CR 704.3). Both move to their owners' graveyards.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after combat damage resolves and SBAs have been applied. Stack is empty → advance to EndOfCombat step.

actions.description: After combat damage and SBAs: p2 at 40 life (no player damage — attacker was blocked), both creatures in graveyard
actions.note: CR 510.1c: Llanowar Elves (blocked) assigned all 1 damage to the blocker (Elvish Mystic), not to p2. p2 life remains 40. Both creatures have lethal damage: SBA 704.5g destroyed both and moved them to their owners' graveyards.
